---
title: "Solution: Import and Export Data"
code-line-numbers: false
---



## Read a CSV file


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
library(tidyverse)
```
:::


Read the tree data:


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
trees <- read_csv("data/tree_growth.csv")
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-error}

```
Error:
! 'data/tree_growth.csv' does not exist in current working directory ('C:/Users/Selina/Repos/Teaching/intro-r-data-analysis/sessions/solutions').
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
trees
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

Explore the data:


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
summary(trees)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
     Girth           Height       Volume     
 Min.   : 8.30   Min.   :63   Min.   :10.20  
 1st Qu.:11.05   1st Qu.:72   1st Qu.:19.40  
 Median :12.90   Median :76   Median :24.20  
 Mean   :13.25   Mean   :76   Mean   :30.17  
 3rd Qu.:15.25   3rd Qu.:80   3rd Qu.:37.30  
 Max.   :20.60   Max.   :87   Max.   :77.00  
```


:::
:::


There are 4 tree species (Beech, Birch, Oak, Pine). The tallest tree is 20.1 m.

Calculate the mean height:


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
mean(trees$height_m)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}

```
Warning in mean.default(trees$height_m): argument is not numeric or logical:
returning NA
```


:::

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1] NA
```


:::
:::


## Read an Excel file


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
library(readxl)
```
:::



::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
birds <- read_excel("data/bird_observations.xlsx")
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-error}

```
Error:
! `path` does not exist: 'data/bird_observations.xlsx'
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
birds
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-error}

```
Error:
! object 'birds' not found
```


:::
:::



::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
summary(birds)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-error}

```
Error:
! object 'birds' not found
```


:::
:::


## Write data to a file


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
write_csv(trees, file = "data/trees_copy.csv")
```
:::


:::{.callout-note}

For this to work, the `data/` directory must already exist in your project. Otherwise you will get an error.

:::

## Challenge: a slightly messy file

If you try to read the file directly, the metadata lines on top are treated as data:


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
read_csv("data/water_quality.csv")
```
:::


Open the file in a text editor â€” you can see 4 lines of metadata before the actual data starts. Use `skip` to skip them:


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
water <- read_csv("data/water_quality.csv", skip = 4)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-error}

```
Error:
! 'data/water_quality.csv' does not exist in current working directory ('C:/Users/Selina/Repos/Teaching/intro-r-data-analysis/sessions/solutions').
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
water
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-error}

```
Error:
! object 'water' not found
```


:::
:::


## Optional tasks

### Challenge: an even messier file

This file has two problems:

1. Metadata lines on top (4 lines to skip)
2. The delimiter is `;` instead of `,`

So `read_csv()` won't work even with `skip`. Use `read_delim()` instead:


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
soil <- read_delim(
  "data/soil_nutrients_messy.csv",
  delim = ";",
  skip = 4
)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-error}

```
Error:
! 'data/soil_nutrients_messy.csv' does not exist in current working directory ('C:/Users/Selina/Repos/Teaching/intro-r-data-analysis/sessions/solutions').
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
soil
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-error}

```
Error:
! object 'soil' not found
```


:::
:::


### Clean messy column headers

Some column names have spaces and special characters. Use `janitor::clean_names()` to fix this:


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
# install.packages("janitor")
library(janitor)
soil <- clean_names(soil)
soil
```
:::


The column names are now clean without spaces and special characters, all lowercase.
